title: Apply for a CARICOM–Costa Rica certificate of origin
description: Get a certificate of origin under the CARICOM–Costa Rica Free Trade Agreement.
summary: The Free Trade Agreement between CARICOM and Costa Rica gives qualifying goods preferential customs treatment when they move between the two markets. The Certificate of Origin is the proof that your goods qualify.
Unlike a single-shipment certificate, this one can cover a period of time. You set the period on the form, and the certificate covers every shipment of the listed goods during that period.
You need to identify the producer of the goods, if it is not the same as the exporter. You also need to give the HS tariff code for each item and confirm which origin criterion applies to it.
BIDC reviews and issues the certificate. If a customs authority in Costa Rica later queries the origin of the goods, BIDC may be asked to confirm what they certified.

items: the producer’s name and address, if different
the period the certificate will cover
the goods description, HS tariff code, origin criterion and value

items.name: Bill of lading or airway bill
Proof that the goods meet the CARICOM–Costa Rica rules of origin

how_long: Certificates are usually issued within 3 to 5 working days.

options.heading: In person
content: Take the completed CARICOM–Costa Rica certificate of origin to BIDC at Pelican House.

contact.address:
Pelican House, Princess Alice Highway
St. Michael

assumptions: ‘Certificates are usually issued within 3 to 5 working days’ is an estimate.
‘Covers a period of time’ is a common feature of free-trade-agreement certificates; confirm the exact wording on the current form.
